title: Search shows wrong results — finding archived tasks instead of active ones
body: Something is wrong with search. When I search for tasks, I'm getting results that include old archived tasks that should be hidden, and I'm not seeing some active tasks that I know exist. For example, I searched for 'Q2 planning' and got an archived task from last year but not the active one I created yesterday. This is happening for all my searches. My teammate says she sees the same thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — search returning stale/wrong results is definitely disruptive.

To start narrowing this down: **was search working correctly for you before, and if so, when did you first notice the problem?** Specifically, did anything change around that time — a product update, a change in how you organize tasks, or anything else you can think of?

Also, are you using TaskFlow on web, desktop, or mobile?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yes, search was definitely working fine before. I first noticed the problem about three days ago, right after the v2.3.1 update was applied. I remember because we had a patch/migration that ran, and pretty much immediately after that my search results started looking wrong. I use TaskFlow on the web. The timing lines up exactly with that update — it was fine the day before and broken right after.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks — that's very helpful. The tight correlation with the v2.3.1 migration is a strong signal.

Here's what I need to narrow down next: **when you click on one of those old archived tasks that appears in search results, what status does it show?** Is it still marked as "archived" in the task detail view, or does it appear as "active"?

Similarly, the active 'Q2 planning' task you created yesterday — if you navigate to it directly (not through search), does it show up normally in your task list?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Good question. When I click on one of those archived tasks that shows up in search, it still shows as 'archived' in the task detail view — so the task itself knows it's archived, it's just showing up in search when it shouldn't be. And yes, my active 'Q2 planning' task shows up perfectly fine when I go to my task list directly without using search. It's right there, marked active, everything looks normal. It's only when I search that things are wrong — search seems to be pulling up archived tasks and hiding active ones. It's almost like the filter is flipped or something.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That observation about the filter being 'flipped' is a great clue — I want to test that theory.

In the search interface, **is there a filter or toggle for showing/hiding archived tasks** (something like a checkbox, dropdown, or sidebar option)? If so, what is it currently set to?

Also, if you try explicitly toggling that filter (if it exists), does the behavior change at all — e.g., do the correct active tasks suddenly appear?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yes, there's a filter for archived tasks in the search interface — by default it's set to 'Active tasks only,' which should hide archived ones. When I toggle it to 'Archived only,' I actually see my active tasks instead! And if I switch it back to 'Active only,' I get the archived ones again. So you're right, it really does seem like the filter is completely inverted. It's like 'active' and 'archived' got swapped somewhere. If I set it to 'All tasks' I see everything, but it's a confusing mix since I have about 300 archived tasks mixed in with my 150 active ones.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Search filter for active/archived tasks is inverted since v2.3.1 migration","problem":"After the v2.3.1 update and its associated migration, the search filter that controls active vs. archived task visibility is completely inverted. Selecting 'Active tasks only' returns archived tasks, and selecting 'Archived only' returns active tasks. The underlying task statuses are correct — this is purely a search/filter-layer issue.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The v2.3.1 migration likely swapped the flag values or enum mappings that the search index uses to distinguish active from archived tasks. The task records themselves retain the correct status (confirmed by viewing task detail), so the corruption is isolated to either the search index entries or the filter predicate logic. Most likely the migration inverted a boolean flag (e.g., `is_archived` written as `true` for active tasks and `false` for archived) or swapped enum values when re-indexing.","reproduction_steps":["Apply the v2.3.1 update/migration to a TaskFlow instance with both active and archived tasks","Open TaskFlow web and use the search feature to search for any term matching both active and archived tasks (e.g., 'Q2 planning')","Observe that with the default 'Active tasks only' filter, archived tasks appear instead of active ones","Toggle the filter to 'Archived only' and observe that active tasks appear instead","Toggle to 'All tasks' and observe both are present but the status labels on individual tasks are correct"],"environment":"TaskFlow web client, v2.3.1 (post-migration). Confirmed on at least two user accounts.","severity":"high","impact":"All users on the web client who use search are affected. Search is functionally broken for anyone relying on the default 'Active tasks only' filter, which is the default experience. Users with many archived tasks (reporter has ~300 archived vs 150 active) see mostly irrelevant results. Workaround exists — users can manually invert the filter or use 'All tasks' — but it is confusing and error-prone.","recommended_fix":"Investigate the v2.3.1 migration script for changes to the search index schema or re-indexing logic. Look for an inverted boolean or swapped enum values in the active/archived flag written to the search index. Fix the migration (or write a corrective migration) to re-index tasks with the correct flag values. Also audit the search filter predicate itself in case the inversion is in the query layer rather than the data layer.","proposed_test_case":"Create a test with a mix of active and archived tasks. Run the v2.3.1 migration. Assert that a search query with the 'Active tasks only' filter returns only tasks with status=active, and 'Archived only' returns only tasks with status=archived. Additionally, add a regression test to the search filter logic that verifies the filter predicate maps 'Active tasks only' to the correct index value.","information_gaps":["Exact contents of the v2.3.1 migration script (requires codebase inspection)","Whether the search index is a separate data store (e.g., Elasticsearch) or queries the primary database directly","Whether other filter dimensions (e.g., by project, by assignee) are also affected or only the active/archived flag"]}
